definition: The yield curve's informational content is vol-regime-dependent: in low-vol regimes it reflects expectations (reliable recession signal); in high-vol regimes term premium noise contaminates the signal. The 2019 inversion (expectations-driven, MOVE low, correctly predicted slowdown) vs. 2022-23 inversion (term premium compressed by 80-120bp, MOVE high, did not produce recession on schedule) is a clean natural experiment.
evidence: 2019 inversion: expectations-driven MOVE low correctly predicted slowdown
2022-23 inversion: term premium compressed by 80-120bp MOVE high did not produce recession on schedule
Term premium explains 50-65% of 10Y variation in ACM models when regressed on MOVE
Pair_3 debate rated this as strongest single analytical point in either paper
open_questions: Circularity risk — both MOVE and term premium models draw from yield curve inputs
In the current high-floor MOVE regime how much should curve signals be discounted?
Can the expectations vs term premium decomposition be done in real time with sufficient precision?
